nist-csf-v2-gv-oc-01.requirements: The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management

nist-csf-v2-gv-oc-02.requirements: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, and their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity risk management are understood and considered

nist-csf-v2-gv-oc-03.requirements: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity - including privacy and civil liberties obligations - are understood and managed

nist-csf-v2-gv-oc-04.requirements: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that stakeholders depend on or expect from the organization are understood and communicated

nist-csf-v2-gv-oc-05.requirements: Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on are understood and communicated

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-01.requirements: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-02.requirements: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-03.requirements: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-04.requirements: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-05.requirements: Lines of communication across the organization are established for cybersecurity risks, including risks from suppliers and other third parties

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-06.requirements: A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing cybersecurity risks is established and communicated

nist-csf-v2-gv-rm-07.requirements: Strategic opportunities (i.e., positive risks) are characterized and are included in organizational cybersecurity risk discussions

nist-csf-v2-gv-rr-01.requirements: Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for cybersecurity risk and fosters a culture that is risk-aware, ethical, and continually improving

nist-csf-v2-gv-rr-02.requirements: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced

nist-csf-v2-gv-rr-03.requirements: Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies

nist-csf-v2-gv-rr-04.requirements: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices

nist-csf-v2-gv-po-01.requirements: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced

nist-csf-v2-gv-po-02.requirements: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced to reflect changes in requirements, threats, technology, and organizational mission

nist-csf-v2-gv-ov-01.requirements: Cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed to inform and adjust strategy and direction

nist-csf-v2-gv-ov-02.requirements: The cybersecurity risk management strategy is reviewed and adjusted to ensure coverage of organizational requirements and risks

nist-csf-v2-gv-ov-03.requirements: Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments needed

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-01.requirements: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-02.requirements: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established, communicated, and coordinated internally and externally

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-03.requirements: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management is integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, risk assessment, and improvement processes

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-04.requirements: Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-05.requirements: Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts and other types of agreements with suppliers and other relevant third parties

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-06.requirements: Planning and due diligence are performed to reduce risks before entering into formal supplier or other third-party relationships

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-07.requirements: The risks posed by a supplier, their products and services, and other third parties are understood, recorded, prioritized, assessed, responded to, and monitored over the course of the relationship

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-08.requirements: Relevant suppliers and other third parties are included in incident planning, response, and recovery activities

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-09.requirements: Supply chain security practices are integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management programs, and their performance is monitored throughout the technology product and service life cycle

nist-csf-v2-gv-sc-10.requirements: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management plans include provisions for activities that occur after the conclusion of a partnership or service agreement

nist-csf-v2-id-am-01.requirements: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained

nist-csf-v2-id-am-02.requirements: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained

nist-csf-v2-id-am-03.requirements: Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained

nist-csf-v2-id-am-04.requirements: Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained

nist-csf-v2-id-am-05.requirements: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission

nist-csf-v2-id-am-07.requirements: Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained

nist-csf-v2-id-am-08.requirements: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-01.requirements: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-02.requirements: Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-03.requirements: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-04.requirements: Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-05.requirements: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response prioritization

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-06.requirements: Risk responses are chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-07.requirements: Changes and exceptions are managed, assessed for risk impact, recorded, and tracked

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-08.requirements: Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-09.requirements: The authenticity and integrity of hardware and software are assessed prior to acquisition and use

nist-csf-v2-id-ra-10.requirements: Critical suppliers are assessed prior to acquisition

nist-csf-v2-id-im-02.requirements: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties

nist-csf-v2-id-im-03.requirements: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities

nist-csf-v2-id-im-04.requirements: Incident response plans and other cybersecurity plans that affect operations are established, communicated, maintained, and improved

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-01.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-01.requirements: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-02.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-02.requirements: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-03.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-03.requirements: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-04.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-04.requirements: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-05.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-05.requirements: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-06.control: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Requirements
nist-csf-v2-pr-aa-06.requirements: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk

nist-csf-v2-pr-at-01.requirements: Personnel are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

nist-csf-v2-pr-at-02.requirements: Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform relevant tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

nist-csf-v2-pr-ds-01.requirements: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected

nist-csf-v2-pr-ds-02.requirements: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected

nist-csf-v2-pr-ds-10.requirements: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected

nist-csf-v2-pr-ds-11.requirements: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-01.requirements: Configuration management practices are established and applied

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-02.requirements: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-03.requirements: Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-04.requirements: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-05.requirements: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented

nist-csf-v2-pr-ps-06.requirements: Secure software development practices are integrated, and their performance is monitored throughout the software development life cycle

nist-csf-v2-pr-ir-01.requirements: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage

nist-csf-v2-pr-ir-02.requirements: The organization's technology assets are protected from environmental threats

nist-csf-v2-pr-ir-03.requirements: Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations

nist-csf-v2-pr-ir-04.requirements: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained

nist-csf-v2-de-cm-01.requirements: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

nist-csf-v2-de-cm-02.requirements: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events

nist-csf-v2-de-cm-03.requirements: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events

nist-csf-v2-de-cm-06.requirements: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

nist-csf-v2-de-cm-09.requirements: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-02.requirements: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-03.requirements: Information is correlated from multiple sources

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-04.requirements: The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are understood

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-06.requirements: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-07.requirements: Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis

nist-csf-v2-de-ae-08.requirements: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria

nist-csf-v2-rs-ma-01.requirements: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared

nist-csf-v2-rs-ma-02.requirements: Incident reports are triaged and validated

nist-csf-v2-rs-ma-04.requirements: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed

nist-csf-v2-rs-ma-05.requirements: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied

nist-csf-v2-rs-an-03.requirements: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident

nist-csf-v2-rs-an-06.requirements: Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records' integrity and provenance are preserved

nist-csf-v2-rs-an-07.requirements: Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved

nist-csf-v2-rs-an-08.requirements: An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated

nist-csf-v2-rs-co-02.requirements: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents

nist-csf-v2-rs-co-03.requirements: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-01.requirements: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-02.requirements: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-03.requirements: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-04.requirements: Critical mission Sections and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-05.requirements: The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed

nist-csf-v2-rc-rp-06.requirements: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed

nist-csf-v2-rc-co-03.requirements: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders

nist-csf-v2-rc-co-04.requirements: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging
